title: Unable to locate package skopeo on Ubuntu 18.04 & 20.04
body:
Starting having issues pulling Skopeo into my Ubuntu 18.04 images starting last night.  How can we get `skopeo` to install?

```
# apt-get install -y skopeo
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Unable to locate package skopeo
```

comments.body:
I have the same issue. Also, unable to install `podman`, although the error is a little different:

PS: Of course, when using the Kubic APT repo. Issue happens not only on 18.04 but also on 20.04.
Skopeo seems to be entirely gone from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/

For now, just to link the reports together: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1632 is where the installation instructions were removed, and at least https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1528#issuecomment-1041556461 records that the Kubic packages are no longer being updated.

Per https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/install.md , Skopeo is now available in the official Ubuntu repositories.

At this point I’m not sure what that means for older Ubuntu releases, and completely removing the older packages does look a little unintuitive. @lsm5 PTAL.
Especially considering that the number of people using Ubuntu 18.04 and 20.04 completely overwhelms the number of people using 22.04 currently.

For those using skopeo in a docker image, you can statically build it from source like this:

```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.18 AS skopeo-build

WORKDIR /usr/src/skopeo

ARG SKOPEO_VERSION="1.8.0"
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/containers/skopeo/archive/v${SKOPEO_VERSION}.tar.gz" \
  | tar -xzf - --strip-components=1

RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 DISABLE_DOCS=1 make BUILDTAGS=containers_image_openpgp GO_DYN_FLAGS=

RUN ./bin/skopeo --version


FROM scratch AS skopeo-rootfs

COPY --from=skopeo-build /usr/src/skopeo/bin/skopeo /usr/local/bin/
COPY --from=skopeo-build /usr/src/skopeo/default-policy.json /etc/containers/policy.json


FROM ubuntu:20.04

COPY --from=skopeo-rootfs / /

RUN skopeo --version
```
I understand it's not being updated for 18.04 but why was it completely removed?
I think the title of the PR could be changed to include 20.04 as well.
@mtrmac so what is the suggested workaround to download older versions of skopeo moving forward?

PS: You can use my `Dockerfile` above to also install `skopeo` with the following command:

```console
sudo docker build . --target skopeo-rootfs --output=/
```

It will write two files to your system: `/usr/local/bin/skopeo` and `/etc/containers/policy.json`

Hi all, RE: 18.04, skopeo seems to have been enabled mistakenly because of cri-o sharing the project. The kubic packages (at least in the form they were) were causing more trouble than they were solving. Lack of maintenance + issues on the Kubic infra side such as the hash mismatches were why I removed them. Please build skopeo from source or use the [skopeo container image](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/install.md#container-images)

Follow-up, the packages have now been restored after lots of pushback. I'd like to reiterate that there will be no further updates and maintenance, not even security fixes. So, I encourage everyone to switch to other ways for fetching skopeo.  The builds should be available now, let me know if anything. 

Sorry about the hassle.
A small addition to @felipecrs comments: you need [BuildKit enabled](https://docs.docker.com/build/buildkit/#getting-started) to use the `--output` flag.

Is there any chance we can have the `default-policy.json` bundled in the binary to avoid having to install two files to the system?

EDIT: Sent a PR: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2014
